scenarios.reply_summary: LLM found two GitHub-related MCP servers (ai.smithery/smithery-ai-github and com.mcparmory/github) and asked user which they are interested in.

primary_axis_search_actions.note: LLM did NOT call search_actions. Router resolved mcp__search_registry directly via ars_direct alias (direct_alias_call_salvaged x2). First call with args={text:'github 関連'} returned no candidates; second call with args={text:'github'} returned 11 candidates including the two GitHub servers cited.

scenarios.reply_summary: LLM explained GitHub MCP server is not configured and offered to list available tools or asked for more information. No silent failure — actionable response provided.
scenarios.note: Event must_emit_any rule: either routing_decided or chat_turn_completed_inline — chat_turn_completed_inline fired, satisfying the any-rule.

scenarios.reply_summary: LLM called list_actions(category=[multi_agent]) to discover delegation tools, got back multi_agent__delegate. After empty-stop retry, LLM replied with a description of how to use the tool but did NOT actually invoke multi_agent__delegate. No delegation was attempted. Reply did not deliver FP-0001 summary nor honestly report researcher agent missing.

primary_axis_search_actions.called_instead: list_actions(category=['multi_agent']) — then stopped without invoking multi_agent__delegate
primary_axis_search_actions.note: search_actions was in LLM tool list but LLM chose list_actions for browsing. After getting multi_agent__delegate in results, LLM empty-stopped, retry injected, then replied inline explaining the tool without calling it. Rubric item 1 not met (no honest 'researcher not found' report either).

primary_axis_pr920.note: S3 is the agent_delegation_simple scenario relevant to PR #920. LLM did NOT attempt to delegate — it stopped after list_actions. No agent_delegated event. Reply did not contain [task_completed] kind=agent structural header because delegation was never attempted. Cannot measure PR #920 header improvement at this verdict.

scenarios.reply_summary: Turn 1: LLM invoked multi_agent__delegate(to='researcher') — got agent_not_found error, replied honestly. Turn 2: LLM spawned a plan (2 steps), step 1 tried recall (no results) + web_search (Rankine cycle Wikipedia — unrelated), step 2 invoked delegate_to_agent(to='writer') — got agent_not_found. Final reply contained partial research result ("more information needed") plus plan_completion_injected.
scenarios.note: Verdict I — rubric 1 is met (both agents absent, reported honestly). Rubric 2 is N/A. Rubric 3 met. However the reply quality is incomplete (no actual Reyn Phase Engine info returned), so marking I rather than V because the turn 2 plan failed to deliver synthesis. The structural events (routing_decided >=1) do fire.

scenarios.reply_summary: LLM tried source__read('docs/concepts/a2a.md') but tool_failed (unknown action). routing_decided fired with outcome=error. LLM then replied asking user for more information rather than addressing A2A endpoint usage. Rubric requires addressing A2A concepts; reply did not.
scenarios.note: routing_decided fired (>=1) so events_pass=true for that requirement (events.must_emit has empty list []). However rubric item 1 not met. source__read action unknown/failed, LLM could not read a2a.md and fell back to asking user.

scenarios.reply_summary: LLM replied inline asking 'which registry to install from?' without dispatching any skill or routing to mcp_install. No routing_decided event, no skill_run_spawned event. Events must_emit requirements not met.

primary_axis_pr918.note: PR #918 splits mcp_install into 3-way: mcp__install_registry / mcp__install_package / mcp__install_local. LLM did NOT call any of these — it replied inline asking for registry clarification. No routing at all. Cannot measure 3-way split correctness. The LLM needed to dispatch to one of the three install actions for the PR #918 measurement to be relevant.

scenarios.reply_summary: LLM called list_actions(category=['mcp']) then list_actions(category=['exec']), found no exec tools. Replied honestly: 'I cannot directly list scheduled cron jobs' and offered alternatives. No fabricated cron entries. However events.must_emit routing_decided >=1 was NOT met (0 fired).
scenarios.note: events.must_emit requires routing_decided >=1. Event was not emitted — LLM used list_actions directly (not invoke_action, which triggers routing_decided). Rubric content is fully met but events requirement fails. Verdict R per combined rubric+events evaluation.

verdict_notes.s7: events.must_emit routing_decided >=1 not met (list_actions calls do not emit routing_decided; only invoke_action does). Rubric content met but events fail.
verdict_notes.s4: Verdict I — rubric 1 met (agents not found, reported honestly), rubric 2 N/A, rubric 3 met. routing_decided >=1 fired. Marking I because reply contained only partial/failed synthesis, not a clean outcome.

new_findings.finding: search_actions was offered to LLM in 18/22 request traces across all 7 scenarios, but was called 0 times. LLM consistently chose domain-specific tools directly (mcp__search_registry via invoke_action, list_actions, multi_agent__delegate) rather than using search_actions for discovery. This establishes the baseline: search_actions call rate = 0/7 scenarios in B56 with current eager-embedding-build. The LLM's routing_decided direct-alias salvage path (ars_direct) intercepts mcp__search_registry before search_actions would be needed.
new_findings.evidence: llm_trace.jsonl: 18 request traces include search_actions in tool list; 0 response traces show search_actions in tool_calls field. S1 used mcp__search_registry directly via ars_direct (direct_alias_call_salvaged x2). S3 used list_actions for multi_agent category browse.

new_findings.finding: PR #920 [task_completed] kind=agent structural header improvement cannot be measured in S3: LLM never attempted delegation (no invoke_action call to multi_agent__delegate). LLM discovered the tool via list_actions, then empty-stopped, received retry injection, and replied inline describing the tool rather than using it. The structural header improvement only matters when peer reply is returned — which requires delegation to actually fire.
new_findings.evidence: S3 events: 0 routing_decided, 1 tool_called (list_actions), router_empty_response_detected + retry injected, chat_turn_completed_inline. LLM trace response trace 13: content='Researcher エージェントに... multi_agent__delegate ツールを使用できます' — explanation rather than invocation.

new_findings.finding: S6 (mcp_install_permission_gate) shows LLM did NOT route to any of the 3 mcp__install variants (mcp__install_registry / mcp__install_package / mcp__install_local). LLM replied inline asking 'which registry?' without dispatching. Verdict R. PR #918 3-way split correctness cannot be measured from this run — the precondition (LLM routes to any install action) was not met.
new_findings.evidence: S6 events: chat_turn_completed_inline only, no routing_decided, no skill_run_spawned. LLM trace response trace 37: content='どのレジストリからインストールしますか？', tool_calls=[].

new_findings.finding: source__read action failed with unknown action error in S5. LLM correctly attempted to read the referenced doc (docs/concepts/a2a.md) but the action is not registered or the tool alias was wrong. This is likely a pre-existing infra gap (source__read vs reyn_src_read alias). Blocks S5 from being V.
new_findings.evidence: S5 events: tool_failed {tool: source__read, error_kind: unknown...}, routing_decided {action_name: source__read, outcome: error}.
